_metadata.description: Explicit exoneration allowlist for SAME_UNII_DIFFERENT_NAMES audit findings. Each entry documents a UNII that maps to multiple reference entries which are legitimately the same FDA-cataloged compound (common vs Latin name, branded form vs generic, active vs inactive role, etc.). Listed here, the audit emits 'review' severity instead of 'critical', and the Sprint 1 UNII-first matching path is safe to land because runtime priority order (banned > IQM > standardized > botanical > other) resolves correctly.

field_contract.fda_canonical_name: Canonical name from fda_unii_cache.json::unii_to_name — the FDA-authoritative substance name
field_contract.entries: List of {file, entry_id} that share this UNII
field_contract.rationale: Plain-English explanation of why these entries legitimately share the same FDA UNII

_metadata.audit_rule: Per pre-Sprint-1 blocker rule (docs/UNII_TRIAGE_2026_05_14.md): no SAME_UNII_DIFFERENT_NAMES critical finding may remain in the audit's post-exoneration output unless it is in this allowlist with rationale + FDA canonical name + regression test (scripts/tests/test_unii_exoneration_allowlist.py).

exonerations.rationale: Both are branded mannitol excipients (Parteck = Merck, Pearlitol = Roquette). Same FDA-cataloged compound, different brand names. Single tier (other_ingredients), so runtime collision logging will warn but the resolved payload is correct either way.
Common name (Winged Treebine) vs Latin binomial (Cissus quadrangularis) for the same plant. Standardized entry wins for active-side scoring.
All maritime pine (Pinus pinaster) bark extract. Pycnogenol is the Horphag-branded standardized form. IQM wins for active scoring.

All derived from FDA-cataloged Citrus aurantium dulcis fruit powder. Crystals and flavor are downstream products of the same orange fruit. Botanical wins for scoring; other entries route to inactive recognition.
Common Chinese name (Danshen) vs Latin binomial (Salvia miltiorrhiza) for the same plant. Standardized entry wins for active scoring.
Same compound (DMG, N,N-dimethylglycine), IQM is active-scoring entry, other_ingredients is inactive-recognition entry. Different roles, identical chemistry. IQM wins at runtime.
Latin binomial (Kaempferia parviflora) vs common name (Black Ginger) for the same plant. IQM wins.

All Zingiber officinale (ginger) entries. Intentional separate modeling for scoring context: raw root vs standardized extract vs flavor preparation. Priority order resolves to IQM for active scoring; other tiers route inactive disclosures.
Same compound (CLA), two roles. IQM is the active-scoring entry; other_ingredients is the inactive-recognition entry for CLA oil used as a carrier. IQM wins.
Same spice (Syzygium aromaticum / Eugenia caryophyllus), two contexts (whole/herb vs powdered formulation aid). Botanical wins for scoring.
Latin binomial (Isatis tinctoria) vs common name (Woad) for the same plant. IQM wins.

Vitamin C IS ascorbic acid (same compound). Two roles: IQM models it as an active vitamin; other_ingredients models it as a preservative additive. IQM wins for active scoring; other tier handles inactive disclosure as a preservative.
